description: Canonical schema for Parquet files in the PIP Master Arrow Repository. Contract between {pipdata} and {piptm}. Column names reflect {pipdata} clean output where possible, with minimal renames documented in source_column.

columns.name: country_code
columns.description: ISO3 country code. Not present as a data column — injected as a constant from metadata$country_code. Must match the partition directory value (country=<country_code>). All rows in a single Parquet file share the same value.

columns.name: surveyid_year
columns.description: Survey identification year. Not present as a data column — injected as a constant from metadata$surveyid_year. May differ from survey_year for multi-round surveys. Used as the year partition key. All rows in a single Parquet file share the same value.

columns.name: welfare_type
columns.description: Welfare aggregate type. Not present as a data column — injected as a constant from metadata$welfare_type. Raw metadata value is a full string ('income', 'consumption'); recode to uppercase code required ('INC', 'CON'). Must match the partition directory value (welfare=<welfare_type>). All rows in a single Parquet file share the same value.

columns.name: version
columns.description: Combined master and alternative version string derived from metadata$vermast and metadata$veralt (e.g. 'v01_v04'). Atomic value — never split into components at the partition level. Must match the partition directory value (version=<version>). All rows in a single Parquet file share the same value.

columns.name: pip_id
columns.description: File-level survey identifier (e.g. 'BOL_2020_EH_INC_ALL'). Uniquely identifies a single Parquet file — one welfare type for one survey. Stored in the data column so the file is self-describing. Derived from the release inventory pip_id column. Used as the Parquet filename stem.

columns.name: survey_acronym
columns.description: Short survey name (e.g. EH, LSMS, GEIH). Not present as a data column — injected as a constant from metadata$survey_acronym. Stored for human readability in downstream outputs.

columns.name: welfare
columns.description: PPP-adjusted welfare (consumption or income). Present as a data column. The {pipdata} ALL module provides this already PPP-adjusted in the 'welfare' column. Zero values are permitted (valid zero-income observations). Negative values are not permitted.

columns.name: weight
columns.description: Individual-level sampling weight. Must be strictly positive. Used as-is from the data column 'weight'. Assumed to be clean and valid after {pipdata} harmonization.

columns.name: gender
columns.description: Individual gender. Present as a data column. Must be absent (column omitted, not all-NA) if the survey does not include this dimension. Preferred source is 'gender' (string: 'male'/'female'). Fallback is 'male' (integer: 1=male, 0=female). Standardise to lowercase string before writing. Factor levels are fixed: ['male', 'female'].

columns.name: area
columns.description: Urban/rural classification. Present as a data column. Must be absent (column omitted) if not available. Preferred source is 'area' (string: 'urban'/'rural'). Fallback is 'urban' (integer: 1=urban, 0=rural). Standardise to lowercase string before writing. Factor levels are fixed: ['urban', 'rural'].

columns.name: educat4
columns.description: Educational attainment — 4-category GMD classification. Present as a data column when available in the survey. Factor levels are survey-specific (GMD verbose labels preserved as-is, e.g. 'Primary (complete or incomplete)'). Column is absent when not available.

columns.name: educat5
columns.description: Educational attainment — 5-category GMD classification. Present as a data column when available in the survey. Factor levels are survey-specific. Column is absent when not available.

columns.name: educat7
columns.description: Educational attainment — 7-category GMD classification. Present as a data column when available in the survey. Factor levels are survey-specific. Column is absent when not available.

columns.name: age
columns.description: Age of the individual in years. Present as a data column. Stored as a continuous integer, not a categorical. Age-group binning (e.g. 0-14, 15-24, 25-64, 65+) is performed by {piptm} at query time, not at ingestion. Must be absent (column omitted) if not available in the survey. Zero is a valid age (infants).

filename_convention.description: The welfare_type suffix in survey_id is replaced: _PIP (and any trailing module suffix) becomes _<welfare_type>. Example: ALB_2012_LSMS_V01_M_V04_A_PIP_TB-ALL becomes ALB_2012_LSMS_V01_M_V04_A_INC-0.parquet

schema_rules: Partition key columns (country_code, surveyid_year, welfare_type, version) must be stored inside the Parquet file in addition to encoding the partition path — this enables self-describing validation.
The version column encodes metadata$vermast and metadata$veralt as a single atomic string (e.g. 'v01_v04'). It must never be split into components at the partition level.

Optional breakdown dimension columns must be ABSENT from the file (column not present, not present as all-NA) when not available for a survey. Presence in the file implies availability and must be reflected in the manifest available_dimensions field.
educat4, educat5, and educat7 are stored with their original GMD factor levels — no recoding to a canonical 4-level set is applied. Level sets are survey-specific.
Only columns defined in this schema may be written to the Parquet file. Additional columns from {pipdata} clean output must be dropped before write.
'age' is stored as a continuous int32, not a categorical/dictionary column. Age-group binning is performed at query time by {piptm}.
Zero welfare values are permitted and must not be dropped. They represent valid zero-income observations.
Cleaning and standardization rules are fully specified in docs/pre-arrow-cleaning-spec.md. The schema defines the contract; the spec defines how to reach it.
